issue_title: Any tests using PhantomJS failing in some newer linux environments
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

After setup new laptop, Any test using PhantomJS is failing for me with the error like this:

```
09:01:26,169 INFO [org.keycloak.testsuite.adapter.servlet.DemoServletsAdapterTest] [DemoServletsAdapterTest] testWithoutKeycloakConf() STARTED
Feb 03, 2023 9:01:26 AM org.openqa.selenium.phantomjs.PhantomJSDriverService <init>
INFO: executable: /home/mposolda/IdeaProjects/keycloak/testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/target/drone/1c947d57fce2f21ce0b43fe2ed7cd361/phantomjs-2.1.1-linux-x86_64/bin/phantomjs
Feb 03, 2023 9:01:26 AM org.openqa.selenium.phantomjs.PhantomJSDriverService <init>
INFO: port: 1443
Feb 03, 2023 9:01:26 AM org.openqa.selenium.phantomjs.PhantomJSDriverService <init>
INFO: arguments: [--ignore-ssl-errors=true, --web-security=false, --webdriver=1443, --webdriver-logfile=/home/mposolda/IdeaProjects/keycloak/testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/phantomjsdriver.log]
Feb 03, 2023 9:01:26 AM org.openqa.selenium.phantomjs.PhantomJSDriverService <init>
INFO: environment: {}
Auto configuration failed
139991832651712:error:25066067:DSO support routines:DLFCN_LOAD:could not load the shared library:dso_dlfcn.c:185:filename(libproviders.so): libproviders.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
139991832651712:error:25070067:DSO support routines:DSO_load:could not load the shared library:dso_lib.c:244:
139991832651712:error:0E07506E:configuration file routines:MODULE_LOAD_DSO:error loading dso:conf_mod.c:285:module=providers, path=providers
139991832651712:error:0E076071:configuration file routines:MODULE_RUN:unknown module name:conf_mod.c:222:module=providers
Feb 03, 2023 9:01:46 AM org.openqa.selenium.os.OsProcess checkForError
SEVERE: org.apache.commons.exec.ExecuteException: Process exited with an error: 1 (Exit value: 1)
09:01:46,486 ERROR [org.keycloak.testsuite.adapter.servlet.DemoServletsAdapterTest] [DemoServletsAdapterTest] testWithoutKeycloakConf() FAILED 
```

I am seeing this on Ubuntu 22.04 as well as on RHEL 9.1 virtual machine. Java version is OpenJDK11 or OpenJDK17. 

### Solution

It helps to set this environment variable:
```
export OPENSSL_CONF=/dev/null
```
See for example here for more details https://github.com/nodejs/node/issues/43132

Can we eventually set this variable by default to avoid such issues?

### Version

20.0.3

### How to Reproduce?

For example run this test on Ubuntu 22.04 or on RHEL 9.1 (reproduced with RHEL 9.1 virtual machine). Java version is OpenJDK11 or OpenJDK17. 
```
mvn clean install -Dtest=DemoServletsAdapterTest
```

issue_title: Incorrect pagination when viewing the groups of a user
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

In the `keycloak.v2` theme, when a user has more than 10 groups, and one of the groups has been renamed, it is possible for the pagination in the "Groups" tab of the user details to show certain groups multiple times, and omit other groups entirely.

### Version

20.0.3

### Environment

_No response_

### Expected behavior

Groups `group02`-`group11` show up on the first page, and `group21` shows up on the second page.

### Actual behavior

The first page shows the following groups, in the following order:
```
group02 
group03 
group04 
group05 
group06 
group07 
group08 
group09 
group10 
group11
```
The second page shows the following group:
```
group11
```

Note that `group11` appears both on the first page and the second page, while `group21` does not appear on either page.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Run `docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:20.0.3 start-dev`.
2. Go to localhost:8080/admin and log in as the `admin` user.
3. In the Groups section of the `master` realm, create, in the following order, groups named:
- `group01`
- `group02`
- `group03`
- `group04`
- `group05`
- `group06`
- `group07`
- `group08`
- `group09`
- `group10`
- `group11`
4. In the Users section of the `master` realm, create a user named `testuser`. Before clicking "Create", first click "Join Groups" and select all of the above groups.
5. In the Groups section of the `master` realm, change the name of `group01` to `group21`.
6. In the Users section, click the `testuser` and go to the Groups tab to view the groups of the user.
7. (Ensure the pagination setting is set to "10 per page".)
8. Click the forward arrow in the lower right corner to go to the second page of groups.

### Anything else?

The symptoms of this issue are similar to those in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16725, but for a different part of the admin interface.

When showing the first page, the frontend makes a request to `/admin/realms/master/users/<UUID>/groups?first=0&max=11`, and gets a response containing 11 groups: `group21` first, followed by `group02`-`group11` in numerical order.

issue_title: Update documentation around `View all users` behavior in the new admin console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

Adding an LDAP provider in READ_ONLY mode and "Import users" set to On actually imports every user from the LDAP provider, contrary to the documentation.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

Per the documentation (https://www.keycloak.org/docs/20.0.3/server_admin/#synchronizing-ldap-users-to-keycloak), I am expecting users to be imported into the Keycloak user database only upon the user's first (successful) authentication attempt.

### Actual behavior

All users in the LDAP provider are imported after clicking "Save" to add the provider.

### How to Reproduce?

In a fresh instance, for the master realm, add an LDAP provider (that contains valid users) with the following settings (where "example" is valid for the LDAP instance, in my case a variant of the company name):
"Vendor" set to "Other"
"Connection URL" set to valid url
"Bind DN" set to "uid=lookup,dc=example,dc=com"
"Bind credentials" set to valid password
"Edit mode" set to "READ_ONLY"
"Users DN" set to "ou=People,dc=example,dc=com"
"User object classes" set to "inetOrgPerson"
"Import users" set to On
"Sync Registrations" set to Off
all other settings at default

Click "Save" and then navigate to the "Users" of the realm and enter "*" to search all users.

The expectation is that there are no users at this point.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: New user from identity provider not having attribute mapped to user federation (LDAP)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

I use SAML as an Identity provider, and LDAP (AD) for user federation.
I added two hardcoded-ldap attributes: `userPassword` and `pwdLastSet` as a mapping for the ldap provider.
When I create a new user via the console, these attributes are correctly set.
However, when a user first logs in via SAML, the user is indeed created on LDAP, but these attributes aren't set.


### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

I'd expect  the ldap attributes that I set up on the ldap provider would be written to a new user, regardless if the user creation is manual via console or when it is created after a login via SAML.

### Actual behavior

Creating a user manually results in successful attribute mapping as defined on the ldap mappers. It doesn't work with SAML - the user is created, but the attributes aren't mapped.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create two hardcoded-ldap-attribute mappers, one for `userPassword` (set to `${RANDOM}`), the other for `pwdLastSet` (set to `-1`).
2. Create a new user manually - check on AD, the attributes are set.
3. Now create that user by logging in via an external SAML identity provider - the attributes are not created.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: v20.0.2 attempts to URL decode same string up to 5 times for unclear reasons
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Code was added in v20.0.2 to repeatedly (up to 5 times) URL decode `redirect_uri` before using it:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/da8d61616399d5c4e4c470c16dd6b0bd98f2b656#diff-8a42999224cc18191469006d9c48d36f83917774badfe5b103e259380318043cR158-R193

This is unusual. Generally whether a thing is encoded or not is clearly specified. "Optional" encoding can lead to exciting behavior, and RFC 6749 makes no mention of this: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6749#section-4.1.3

It's quite possible that there was a very good reason to add this, but there's no any clear info that I could find as to why (there's not enough info the the phrase "Avoid path traversal vis double-url encoding of redirect URI") and that's nearly certainly worth a comment, as this seems like quite unusual behaviour.


### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

To read the code and understand why a variable is optionally attempted to be decoded up to 5 times.

### Actual behavior

Read the code and be puzzled.

### How to Reproduce?

Read https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/da8d61616399d5c4e4c470c16dd6b0bd98f2b656#diff-8a42999224cc18191469006d9c48d36f83917774badfe5b103e259380318043cR158-R193

### Anything else?

While I don't think this has specifically caused an issue for us, we've seen unusual behaviour (502 - we're behind Nginx and suspect the request/response size may have increased for some uses) in 20.0.3 (since updating from 20.0.0) so am reviewing the changes. This one seems quite imprecise and left me puzzled.

issue_title: Required actions execution order (session and user required actions)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Hi, when I configured some required actions like so:

- password reset (priority 1)
- verify email (priority 2)
- terms and conditions (priority 3)

When the user triggers a password reset (via forgot password) I expect the following order:

action |context
--------|-------
password reset| session
verify email |user
terms and conditions |user


but instead they execute in the following order:


action        | context
--------|-------
verify email |user
terms and conditions |user
password reset| session


This seems like a bug to me and I looked into the code and it seems to go
wrong  [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/9b01bf382d69b0eb4f3d0defd27beddbac15ba37/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/AuthenticationManager.java#L1160-L1166)

```java
Stream<String> requiredActions = user.getRequiredActionsStream();
// Here all the user required actions are executed, even if there are higher priority actions stored in the session...
Response action = executionActions(session, authSession, request, event, realm, user, requiredActions);
if (action != null) return action;

//... which are executed afterwards
action = executionActions(session, authSession, request, event, realm, user, authSession.getRequiredActions().stream());
if (action != null) return action;

```

One potential way of fixing this would be to concatenate user required actions and session required actions to execute them in one step.

The only obvious difference would be that we can't return early after executing the user actions, but I don't understand when that is actually desired.



### Version

19.0.2

### Expected behavior

When configuring required actions like so:

- password reset (priority 1)
- verify email (priority 2)
- terms and conditions (priority 3)

When the user triggers a password reset (via forgot password) I expect the following order:

action |context
--------|-------
password reset| session
verify email |user
terms and conditions |user

### Actual behavior

but instead they execute in the following order:


action        | context
--------|-------
verify email |user
terms and conditions |user
password reset| session

### How to Reproduce?

This can be reproduced by configuring some required actions like in the example above.

### Anything else?

Previously reported and discussed over here:

- https://groups.google.com/g/keycloak-dev/c/MqpYd7kKa3U
- https://keycloak.discourse.group/t/required-actions-execution-order-session-and-user-required-actions/10689
commit_message:
Always order required actions by priority (regardless of context)

- AuthenticationManager#actionRequired: make sure that the highest prioritized required action is performed first, possibly before the currently requested required action
- AuthenticationManager#nextRequiredAction: make sure that the next action is requested via URL, also based on highest priority (-> requested URL will match actually performed action, unless required actions for the user are changed by a parallel operation)
- add tests to RequiredActionPriorityTest, add helper method for priority setup to ApiUtil (for easier and more robust setup than up-to-now)
- fix test WebAuthnRegisterAndLoginTest - which failed because WebAuthnRegisterFactory (prio 70) is now executed before WebAuthnPasswordlessRegisterFactory (prio 80)

Closes #16873

Signed-off-by: Daniel Fesenmeyer <daniel.fesenmeyer@bosch.com>

issue_title: File map storage: Entities changing their ID (example: clientId change)
issue_body:
### Description

For some entities, the file store uses the name humans refer to them as a file name and as a reference in the YAML files. One of these examples is the clientId.  

When the user changes the clientId in the UI, the current implementation updates the clientId in the YAML file, but doesn't yet change the name of the file. In addition to that, all the references to that client in the file store need updating, for example the client roles (at the time of writing, they are stored in a folder with the clientId, and they also store the clientId in their YAML). 

### Discussion

Current behavior: 
* Create a client with the UI (and save)
* Create a client role (and save)
* Rename the clientId (and save)
* Go to the list of clients -> client appears with the the new clientId
* Pick the new client from the list -> client not found, as the file on disk hasn't been renamed

Expected behavior: 
* After renaming a clientId, the client should be pickable from the list of clients, and the client roles should still be visible. 

Similar problems might appear for other entities.

### Motivation

The file store uses for entities humans refer to a derived ID from a descriptive name of the entity. When that name changes, and therefore its ID, all references need to change. 

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: direct naked impersonation documentation is wrong
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have searched existing issues - [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release ### Area token-exchange ### Describe the bug I tried to implement Keycloak **direct naked impersonation** from documentation, but in the end I got the error: ``` "error": "access_denied", "error_description": "Client not allowed to exchange" ``` I followed all the steps that are in the [Keycloak Direct Naked Impersonation documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/securing_apps/index.html#direct-naked-impersonation). The problem is that the documentation contains an image that is wrong, namely: It should have been a Client Policy with the client **admin-cli** instead of the user **admin**. Perform the corect request: ``` curl -X POST \ -d "client_id=admin-cli" \ -d "client_secret=the client secret" \ --data-urlencode "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange" \ -d "requested_subject=wburke" \ http://0.0.0.0:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token ``` not what you have now ``` curl -X POST \ -d "client_id=starting-client" \ -d "client_secret=the client secret" \ --data-urlencode "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange" \ -d "requested_subject=wburke" \ http://localhost:8080/realms/myrealm/protocol/openid-connect/token ``` In your documentation you started with **admin-cli**, then you used **admin** and in the end you used **starting-client**. These are 3 different variables that you used instead of just one: **admin-cli** Plus for me to have this working I have to setup, inside keycloak admin, the **user-impersonate** also, to have direct naked impersonation to work. ### Version 20.0.3 ### Expected behavior I explained everything above ### Actual behavior I explained everything above ### How to Reproduce? I explained everything above ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: Docs are misleading about mixed domain Kerberos SPNEGO
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release
- [X] I have searched existing issues

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The docs [state](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/20.0.3/server_admin/#_kerberos):

```
5. If the browser has a Kerberos ticket from desktop login, the browser transfers the desktop sign-on information to Keycloak in header Authorization: Negotiate 'spnego-token'. Otherwise, it displays the standard login screen, and the user enters the login credentials.
```

That's not always true and is browser dependent.  Only if a web browser has been configured to participate in `Authorization: Negotiate` then the token will be sent.  If configured, but the token is invalid, then the standard login screen is displayed.

The missing part however, is if not configured, some browsers fall back to an NTLM dialog prompt.  This is often not what you want and difficult to work around.  At the moment Firefox latest (109.0.1) does behave as originally described and will not use NTLM by default (though I think it can be enabled client-side).  Chrome latest (110.0.5481.78) on Windows (but not Linux or MacOS) will prompt for NTLM by default.  It can be [disabled](https://community.microfocus.com/cyberres/netiq-access-management/accessmanager/f/nam_discussions/209874/kerberos-auth-can-the-ntlm-dialog-be-prevented), but requires control of every user's browser.

SPENGO is only useful in a tightly controlled Intranet, however it may be desirable to allow a single Keycloak cluster to serve a mixed environment where internal (Intranet) users are treated to SPENGO login and external users must use alternative means (username and password).

A good explanation of this issue can be found [here](https://knowledge.broadcom.com/external/article/202739/kerberos-fallback-to-form-chain-authenti.html).  The TLDR: Instead of adding a `Kerberos` www-authenticate [scheme](https://www.iana.org/assignments/http-authschemes/http-authschemes.xhtml) which would allow servers to specify exactly what auth types are allowed, Microsoft created [Negotiate](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4559.txt) instead, which bundles Kerberos and NTLM together such that NTLM is an implied fallback (though browsers can disable NTLM to avoid it).

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

Docs should give clearer picture of SPNEGO behavior and support in Keycloak.  It's clumsy at best outside of an Intranet and if attempting a mixed domain environment beware no standard solution exists.   Detecting client IPs at the load balancer and redirecting to Keycloak using an idp_hint or [prompt=login](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/8989) parameter may be one hack.  Another is providing an explicit separate link to try SPNEGO for users to select on the login page or in each app.

### Actual behavior

Docs leave out some important details and considerations.

### How to Reproduce?

Read docs.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: upgrading from v18.0.2 to 19.0.3 or 20.0.3 fails with ERROR duplicate key value violates unique constraint "constraint_3c"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Dear keycloak team,

we have been using dockerised versions of keycloak for some time. At the moment, we are using version 16.1.1 across the board.
An update to version 18.0.2 was easily possible. When updating to version 19.0.3 or 20.0.3, we received the following error message on one of our instances.

`2023-02-XX HH:MM:SS.521 UTC [36] STATEMENT:  INSERT INTO public.client_attributes (CLIENT_ID, NAME, VALUE) VALUES ('xx40xxxx-3b12-4525-xxxx-xxxcabxxxx6a', 'post.logout.redirect.uris', '+')`

The analysis showed that this client had already set the value. This was due to the fact that the client was provided to us by a partner, which was already running on version 20.0.x. We checked the JSON for the import and it already contained this attribute and so it was transferred to the database. However, the value cannot be found in the graphical overview of version 16.1.1 and 18.0.2.

If an update to a version >18.0.2 is now carried out, it fails with the error mentioned above. To verify the error, we exported the client, deleted the line, imported it again and carried out another update. This was successful and the attribute was set.

### Version

16.1.1 > 18.0.2 > 20.0.3

### Expected behavior

- Importing clients from newer version into older versions should be without the attribute
- the GUI should show all attributes
- the update would recognise the already existing value and not try to set it again



### Actual behavior

- the update tries to set a already existing value again
- the GUI doesn't show all attributes
- Importing clients from newer version into older versions write attributes (ghostly) that can't be seen in the GUI




### How to Reproduce?

 - generating a client in a version > 18.0.2 with the attribute 'post.logout.redirect.uris'
 - exporting this client
 - importing in 16.1.1
 - try to update to an version > 18.0.2 

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: CVE-2022-42003 - wildfly-elytron: A Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability in com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind
issue_body:
# Overview

[com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-databind) is a library which contains the general-purpose data-binding functionality and tree-model for Jackson Data Processor. At the moment 

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) in the `_deserializeWrappedValue()` function in `StdDeserializer.java`, due to resource exhaustion when processing deeply nested arrays.

**NOTE:** This vulnerability is only exploitable when the non-default `UNWRAP_SINGLE_VALUE_ARRAYS` feature is enabled.

# Details

Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its intended and legitimate users.

Unlike other vulnerabilities, DoS attacks usually do not aim at breaching security. Rather, they are focused on making websites and services unavailable to genuine users resulting in downtime.

One popular Denial of Service vulnerability is DDoS (a Distributed Denial of Service), an attack that attempts to clog network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines.

When it comes to open source libraries, DoS vulnerabilities allow attackers to trigger such a crash or crippling of the service by using a flaw either in the application code or from the use of open source libraries.

Two common types of DoS vulnerabilities:

-   High CPU/Memory Consumption- An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to take a disproportionate amount of time to process. For example, [commons-fileupload:commons-fileupload](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/security/code-scanning/SNYK-JAVA-COMMONSFILEUPLOAD-30082).
    
-   Crash - An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to crash. For Example, [npm `ws` package](https://snyk.io/vuln/npm:ws:20171108)
   
# Remediation

Upgrade to WildFly Elytron 1.20.3.CR1 or higher. More details:
- https://github.com/wildfly-security/wildfly-elytron/pull/1847
- https://issues.redhat.com/browse/ELY-2493

# References

-   [Chromium Bugs](https://bugs.chromium.org/p/oss-fuzz/issues/detail?id=51020)
-   [Documentation](https://fasterxml.github.io/jackson-databind/javadoc/2.9/com/fasterxml/jackson/databind/DeserializationFeature.html#UNWRAP_SINGLE_VALUE_ARRAYS)
-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-databind/commit/d78d00ee7b5245b93103fef3187f70543d67ca33)
-   [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-databind/issues/3590)

issue_title: UPDATED_PASSWORD user action shouldn't be triggered when login with linked IdP
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

A user account which have an external Identity Provider linked has an UPDATE_PASSWORD action (because his local password was expired).
When the user logins with an external Identity Provider that is linked to his account, keycloak is asking to update his local password eventhough the user haven't userd his local credentials to login. This behaviour is confusing for the user and I think is incorrect since the action should be trigered only when the user use his local credentials (the expired password) to login.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5125146/219498058-7f1a39e6-4a5e-4514-8011-b3959ba556c1.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5125146/219498310-9f9cc492-9c36-45b1-b372-01031c16348a.png)


### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

The UPDATE_PASSWORD action shouldn't be triggered if the user didn't use his password to login. If for example the user uses a linked IdentityProvider to login, keycloak shouldn't ask to update his local password

### Actual behavior

Keycloak is asking the user to update his password eventhough the user didn't use his password to login (the user is using an external linked IdP)

### How to Reproduce?

Having a user with a linked external IdP
Add the UPDATE_PASSWORD required action to the user
Login to the account using the external IdP

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
UPDATED_PASSWORD required-action triggered only when login using password

`UpdatePassword.evaluateTriggers` adds the required-action to the user by evaluating the expiration password policy. Added a check that skips the evaluation if no password used during auth flow. This check uses the value of an auth note set in the `validatePassword` method of the `AbstractUsernameFormAuthenticator`.
Manually adding UPDATED_PASSWORD required-action to the user continues to trigger the action regardless of the authentication method.

Closes #17155

Signed-off-by: graziang <g.graziano94@gmail.com>

issue_title: Internal server error on wrong metrics endpoint when non-applicaiton-root is configured
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

When `http-relative-path` and `quarkus.http.non-application-root-path` are configured differently, then calls to the (wrong) metrics and health endpoints under `http-relative-path` produce an internal server error.

Example:

* `http-relative-path=/auth`
* `quarkus.http.non-application-root-path=/q`

Metrics endpoint is `/q/metrics`, health endpoint is `/q/health` - both work fine.

Calls to `/auth/metrics` and `/auth/health` return http status code `500` (internal server error), but should return `404` (not found) - since these endpoints should not exist.





### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Calls to non-existing endpoints should always return http status code `404` no errors should be logged.

### Actual behavior

Calls to `/metrics` and `/health` under `http-relative-path` return http status code `500` - internal server error.

The log shows this error message:

```
ERROR [io.quarkus.resteasy] (executor-thread-0) org.jboss.resteasy.spi.LoggableFailure: RESTEASY003880: Unable to find contextual data of type: org.keycloak.models.KeycloakSession
```

### How to Reproduce?

Start keycloak using this command:

```bash
./kc.sh \
  -Dquarkus.transaction-manager.default-transaction-timeout=10s \
  start-dev \
  --health-enabled true \
  --http-relative-path=auth \
  --metrics-enabled=true \
  --cache=local \
  --http-enabled=true \
  --hostname-strict=false \
  --hostname-strict-https=false \
  --features=preview \
  -Dquarkus.http.non-application-root-path=/q \
  -Dquarkus.transaction-manager.enable-recovery=true
```

Then run curl requests to see that metrics and health endpoints under `/q` are working as expected:

* `curl -v http://localhost:8080/q/metrics`
* `curl -v http://localhost:8080/q/health`


Request to the endpoints `/auth/metrics` and  `/auth/health` should return `404` but result in internal server error (`500`):

* `curl -v http://localhost:8080/auth/metrics`
* `curl -v http://localhost:8080/auth/health`



### Anything else?

I'm already working on a fix for this.

This is probably the root cause of the error mentioned in #15278.
commit_message:
Avoid internal server error when root path and non-appliation root path are both set and the wrong metrics/health endpoint is called.

Fixes #17166

issue_title: Docker auth: IllegalArgumentException on multiple resource scopes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

I am using keycloak with the docker auth feature as authentication for a docker registry (official registry image). I am building and pushing a new image to this registry which has a base image also located in this registry with kaniko.

This fails with following error:
```
2023-02-19 22:11:57,326 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-0) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Expecting input string to have 3 parts delineated by a ':' character.  Found 5 parts: repository:my-image:pull,push repository:my-base-image:pull
        at org.keycloak.representations.docker.DockerAccess.<init>(DockerAccess.java:47)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.docker.mapper.AllowAllDockerProtocolMapper.transformDockerResponseToken(AllowAllDockerProtocolMapper.java:46)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.docker.DockerAuthV2Protocol.lambda$authenticated$2(DockerAuthV2Protocol.java:117)
```
This is because the parser of the resource scope is only expecting a single resource scope but kaniko sends multiple (one with pull,push for the image being built and one with pull for the base image in the FROM clause in the Dockerfile). This is valid according to the docker spec: https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/auth/scope/#authorization-server-use

"The POST request only takes in a single scope field but **may use a space to separate a list of multiple resource scopes**."



### Version

20.0.3

There is no error: the authentication succeeds and the image is allowed to be pushed to the registry.

The docker auth protocol follows the docker spec.

issue_title: Duplicated set-cookie headers sent causing issues with proxies
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

I'm using the keycloak-js (20.0.3)  plugin for javascript and when I upgrade the keycloak to version 20.0.2 or 20.0.3 fails with a 502 after session expired or deleted manually. 
In the brokens keycloak versions I need to every time clear the cookies in the edge browser. 
When I clear the cookies it works again.
The version 20.0.1 it works well when the session expired the cookies are removed automatically KEYCLOAK_SESSION, etc.

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

When the session is expired or deleted for a user in a realm, it shows the login page without error (for example in version 20.0.1)

### Actual behavior

When the session is expired or deleted for a user in a realm it shows a 502 error until manually clear the cookies KEYCLOAK_SESSION, etc.  the login page with an error 502  (for example in version 20.0.3)

### How to Reproduce?

Use de npm package keycloak-js (20.0.3)  in a web-app try to login in a keycloak-20.0.3 it will not work well after the session is deleted or expired. (But if you clear the cookies or incgonite page yes) In version 20.0.1 it works well without doing nothing.

_No response_

issue_title: the InfoPage after an ExecuteActionsEmail is not localized based on the user's locale
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

In my case an execute-actions-email is sent to a user for them to update their password. After the user clicks on the link in the execute-action-email they will visit an infopage listing the actions to execute followed by the update-password-page. The infopage is translated according to the locale accepted by the browser-request but update-password-page is translated according to the locale set in the user's profile.

If these two locales are not equal the translation changes during the workflow.

### Version

20.0.5

### Expected behavior

The first page should be translated by the user's locale aswell.

### Actual behavior

The first page and the second page are translated differently if the browser-requested locale and the user-profile's locale are not equal. 

### How to Reproduce?

precondition:
- a realm with a working smtp config and two supported languages (for example: german and english)
- a browser without a cookie specifying a locale for keycloak that is set to one of the supported languages (for example: german)

1. create a user with a specific locale (for example: english)
2. trigger an execute-actions-email for the UPDATE_PASSWORD action
3. the email is in english
4. open the link send by mail in the browser
5. the infopage will open in german, the language accepted by the browser
6. the following update-password-page will open in englisch, the use-profile's locale



### Anything else?

I might have already identified the underlying issue: the authenticed user is not loaded into the loginformsprovider in ExecuteActionsActionTokenHandler::handleToken.

I plan to provide a pull-request shortly.
commit_message:
setting the user in ExecuteActionsActionTokenHandler.handleToken to manage user null case in FreeMarkerLoginFormsProvider.createResponse

Closes #17233

Signed-off-by: Rishabh Singh <rishabhsvats@gmail.com>

issue_title: admin-ui: authz viewing of a group requires also view-members permission
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When using fine-grained permissions on a group and only having the view permission, I am unable to open a group in the admin ui.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1793007/220675748-71f1ac93-a518-4e25-b968-2f9534b307f9.png)

### Version

20.0.3

### Environment

_No response_

### Expected behavior

The group is opened and the members tab is hidden, as the user has no permissions do view or manage the members of the group.

### Actual behavior

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1793007/220675748-71f1ac93-a518-4e25-b968-2f9534b307f9.png)

Request failed with status code 403

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a realm `testrealm`
2. Create a user `testuser` with `query-groups` role
3. Add credentials for that user
4. Create a group `A` and enable fine grained permissions for that group
5. In `clients` -> `realm-management` -> `authorization` -> `policies` create a user policy for the `testuser`
6. Assign that policy to the view permission of the group.
7. Log in to the admin console of the `testrealm` with the created `testuser`
8. Open `Groups`
9. Click on group `A`

### Anything else?

Debugging the network traffic reveals that the group details are returned, but then the UI proceeds to request the members of the group which then returns a `403` error. The members should not be requested if the user does not have the permissions to do so (view-/manage-members).
commit_message:
Add access to full group tree.  Fix access for members tab. Add missing (#19423)

props to Access object.
Fixes #17589

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The documentation [outgoinghttp.adoc](docs/guides/src/main/server/outgoinghttp.adoc) gives an example Proxy mapping command line `*\.(google|googleapis)\.com` which is incorrect. It should be `.*\.(google|googleapis)\.com` with a dot at the beginning.

This is a typo in the documentation only.

This is a typo in the documentation only.

This is a typo in the documentation only.

issue_title: Can't accept terms and conditions after upgrade from 20.0.3 to 21.0.0

authentication

saml

### Describe the bug

Facing same issue as  [12008](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12008#issue-1237144963) in latest releases
Sometimes some Service Providers (SAML) hosted on a specific domain implements CORS policy directive to prevent illegal cross-domain acess. When Keycloak is used as an Identity Provider (SAML) to secure access of these Service Providers with POST-binding profile it fails because of default Referrer-Policy set to 'no-referrer' from 11.0.1 Keycloak Version.

Here is a sample

User-Agent -> Service Provider A (a.com) : User wants to access Service Provider A
User-Agent <- Service Provider A (a.com) : Service Provider returns an html resource with form containing SAMLRequest and the action pointing to the Identity Provider B (b.com)
User-Agent -> Identity Provider B (b.com) : User-Agent POSTs (when page is loaded) SAMLRequest form
User-Agent <- Identity Provider B (b.com) : ... After user authentication ... Identity Provider B returns an html with form containing SAMLResponse and the action pointing to the Service Provider A (a.com) + an http header 'Referrer-Policy' with no-referrer' policy set.
User-Agent -> User-Agent : As Identity Provider B tells to the User-Agent it doesn't want the Referer header (and moreover Origin) be valued (no-referrer policy definition), User-Agent remove http header Referer and set Origin http header to null before POSTing SAMLResponse form
User-Agent -> Service Provider A (a.com) : User agent POSTs (when page is loaded) SAMLResponse form. Origin is at this time null.
Service Provider A (a.com) -> Service Provider A (a.com) : The Service Provider A detects a cross-origin invocation (coming from b.com) and check Origin (which is null). null being not permitted, the Service Provider stops the request and returns to the User-Agent an 'invalid CORS request' error response.

### Version

20.0.5 , 21.0.0

### Expected behavior

Referrer-Policy shoud be computed considering the different use case : If the SP has a cross-origin (can be obtain through the ACS URL) we should override the default policy when returning saml-post-form resource to accept cross-origin invocation.



### Actual behavior

Due to Referrer-Policy set to no-referrer, the User-Agent send html form without Referer header nor Origin (which is set to null).



### How to Reproduce?

Create a SP with a domain (a.com) and an IdP with a domain (b.com)
Implements CORS Policy on SP domain
Register SP on IdP
Triggers a 'login' flow.

### Anything else?

Default policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin should be more appropriate.

issue_title: Make LDAP `searchForUsersStream` consistent with other storages
issue_body:
Searching in LDAP works differently than other storages.

When we use the following method:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/923a321a55747d401d87b7958fe0dee81fabe010/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPStorageProvider.java#L368-L393

with an empty `params` argument, it returns an empty result list. This is inconsistent with other storages, where empty params return all users.

issue_title: javax.net.ssl.SSLException exceptions because org.keycloak.adapters.HttpClientBuilder ignores connectionTTL setting
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

The property connectionTTL can be set but it is never used in the org.keycloak.adapters.HttpClientBuilder#build method. This results that connections live forever, resulting in Connection reset Exceptions, when the connection was closed on the server.

The PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager should be created like this:

```
PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager tcm = new PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager(sf.build(), null, null, null, connectionTTL, connectionTTLUnit);
```

If the poolsize is set to 1 its more difficult. Could be fixed by setting the keepAliveStrategy on BasicHttpClientConnectionManager

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

Property connectionTTL  of org.keycloak.adapters.HttpClientBuilder should be used when creating a httpClient

### Actual behavior

Property connectionTTL  of org.keycloak.adapters.HttpClientBuilder is ignored when creating a httpClient

### How to Reproduce?

- Use a code like this to create an httpClient:
```
            HttpClientBuilder httpClientBuilder = new HttpClientBuilder();

            LOGGER.info("Setting timeouts socketTimeoutSeconds={}, connectionTTLSeconds={}, establishConnectionTimeoutSeconds={}",
                    adapterConfig.getSocketTimeoutSeconds(), adapterConfig.getConnectionTtlSeconds(), adapterConfig.getEstablishConnectionTimeoutSeconds());
            httpClientBuilder.socketTimeout(adapterConfig.getSocketTimeoutSeconds(), TimeUnit.SECONDS);
            httpClientBuilder.connectionTTL(adapterConfig.getConnectionTtlSeconds(), TimeUnit.SECONDS);
            httpClientBuilder.establishConnectionTimeout(adapterConfig.getEstablishConnectionTimeoutSeconds(), TimeUnit.SECONDS);
            HttpClient client = httpClientBuilder.build(adapterConfig);
```

- Use this client to fetch tokens from keycloak. 
- Have some component in between that closes idle connection. This happens for example when an AWS NAT Gateway is in between, that closes the connection after  350 seconds with an RST packet
- Try to fetch the token again

=> javax.net.ssl.SSLException: Connection reset

### Anything else?
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49994901/connection-reset-by-keycloaks-java-adapter
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/nat-gateway-troubleshooting.html
commit_message:
Set connection TTL in adapters HttpClientBuilder
closes #17304
